name: Diff Doctor
tagline: Turn messy diffs into clear fixes.
description: Diff Doctor is a mobile developer tool that reads pull request diffs, code review comments, and recent commit history to diagnose why a change is risky or likely to break. It solves the everyday problem of developers being away from their desks when a review thread is heating up, a build just failed, or a teammate asks, "What changed here?" Instead of showing raw code only, the app rewrites the diff into plain-language explanations, highlights likely side effects, and suggests what to test next. A developer can use it on the commute to understand a failing release, while waiting in line to sanity-check a small hotfix, or during a meeting to answer questions without opening a laptop. For example, if a backend endpoint suddenly returns 500s after a refactor, the app can connect the changed method signatures to the API breakage and point to the most probable missing null check. What makes it unique is its focus on translating code changes into actionable debugging narratives, not just viewing files or commenting on reviews like existing Git clients.
features: It converts raw pull request diffs into a plain-English change summary so developers can understand impact quickly on a small screen.
It flags likely regression points by tracing renamed functions, moved logic, and altered dependencies, which helps users spot problems before they reach production.
It links code review comments to the exact changed lines and explains the reviewer concern in simple terms, making remote review conversations easier to follow.
It generates a context-aware test checklist from the diff, so developers know which unit, integration, or manual checks matter most after a change.
It keeps a compact release timeline that shows the last few commits, failing builds, and related tickets, which matters when users need to diagnose an issue in minutes.

audience": "Mobile and backend developers, team leads, and on-call engineers aged roughly 22-45 who regularly review code, fix bugs, or respond to production issues while away from their desktop setup. It is especially useful for distributed teams and engineers who need fast situational awareness during commutes, meetings, or incident response.",
  "category": "developer tools",
  "useCases": [
    "Maya is on her train home when CI turns red, and she opens Diff Doctor to see which file changed the database query and why the new test is failing.",
    "Jordan is in a meeting and gets a review request for a hotfix, so he checks the app to understand whether the refactor could break the payment callback before approving it.",
    "Elena is on call at 2 a.m. and needs to triage a 500 error quickly, so she uses Diff Doctor to connect the latest deploy diff to the exact missing guard clause."
  ]

{
  "name": "Diff Doctor",
  "tagline": "Turn messy diffs into clear fixes.",
  "description": "Diff Doctor is a mobile developer tool that reads pull request diffs, code review comments, and recent commit history to diagnose why a change is risky or likely to break. It solves the everyday problem of developers being away from their desks when a review thread is heating up, a build just failed, or a teammate asks, \"What changed here?\" Instead of showing raw code only, the app rewrites the diff into plain-language explanations, highlights likely side effects, and suggests what to test next. A developer can use it on the commute to understand a failing release, while waiting in line to sanity-check a small hotfix, or during a meeting to answer questions without opening a laptop. For example, if a backend endpoint suddenly returns 500s after a refactor, the app can connect the changed method signatures to the API breakage and point to the most probable missing null check. What makes it unique is its focus on translating code changes into actionable debugging narratives, not just viewing files or commenting on reviews like existing Git clients.",
  "features": [
    "It converts raw pull request diffs into a plain-English change summary so developers can understand impact quickly on a small screen.",
    "It flags likely regression points by tracing renamed functions, moved logic, and altered dependencies, which helps users spot problems before they reach production.",
    "It links code review comments to the exact changed lines and explains the reviewer concern in simple terms, making remote review conversations easier to follow.",
    "It generates a context-aware test checklist from the diff, so developers know which unit, integration, or manual checks matter most after a change.",
    "It keeps a compact release timeline that shows the last few commits, failing builds, and related tickets, which matters when users need to diagnose an issue in minutes."
  ],
  "audience": "Mobile and backend developers, team leads, and on-call engineers aged roughly 22-45 who regularly review code, fix bugs, or respond to production issues while away from their desktop setup.   "category": "developer tools",
  "useCases": [
    "Maya is on her train home when CI turns red, and she opens Diff Doctor to see which file changed the database query and why the new test is failing.",
    "Jordan is in a meeting and gets a review request for a hotfix, so he checks the app to understand whether the refactor could break the payment callback before approving it.",
    "Elena is on call at 2 a.m. and needs to triage a 500 error quickly, so she uses Diff Doctor to connect the latest deploy diff to the exact missing guard clause."
  ]
}
